narrative:
The Zurich Zebras enter today’s slate with significant firepower at the top of the order, headlined by Kyle Schwarber and Christian Yelich, who command high score projections of 85.4 and 81.4 respectively. Schwarber benefits from favorable wind conditions and an elite xwOBA profile against Adrian Houser, while Yelich leverages a massive 13% platoon advantage against Nick Bello to anchor the lineup. Yordan Alvarez remains a must-start despite a slight downgrade due to wind resistance, as his elite base skill and favorable matchup against Feltner keep his ceiling near the top of the roster.

While our core is locked, managers should monitor the risks surrounding Hyeseong Kim and Victor Scott II; both players carry heavy negative modifiers due to lower lineup placement and unfavorable matchups against Scherzer and Littell, respectively, which may hinder our Roto efficiency in runs and RBIs. Trea Turner faces a tactical hurdle with an elite-tier catcher working against his stolen base potential, though his premium lead-off spot mitigates the concern. Furthermore, keep an eye on the wind-affected matchups—Colt Keith and Yordan Alvarez are specifically penalized by incoming winds, necessitating a close watch on potential defensive replacements if the conditions stiffen before first pitch.
